questions.question: What is one of the key advantages of using Complementary Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor (CMOS) technology for digital integrated circuits?
options: Low static power dissipation.
High operating voltage requirements.
Complex fabrication process compared to older technologies.
Inability to scale down to smaller feature sizes.
Exclusive use in analog circuit design.

questions.question: In the Von Neumann architecture or typical CPU design, what is the primary role of the Control Unit?
options: Performing all arithmetic and logical operations on data.
Storing large amounts of program instructions and data.
Generating timing and control signals to manage the execution flow of instructions.
Managing the interaction between the CPU and external I/O devices.
Temporarily holding data for fast access during computations.

questions.question: In advanced VLSI physical design, what phenomenon does "crosstalk" primarily refer to, and why is its analysis crucial?
options: The unintentional magnetic field interference between power and ground lines, leading to voltage fluctuations.
The electrical coupling between adjacent signal lines, where a change in voltage on one line induces a voltage change on another.
The delay variation of clock signals across different parts of the chip due to resistive-capacitive effects.
The thermal degradation of transistor performance at high operating temperatures, leading to reliability issues.
The degradation of signal integrity due to cosmic rays causing single-event upsets in memory cells.

questions.question: What is a fundamental characteristic of a Finite State Machine (FSM) in digital circuit design?
options: Its outputs are solely determined by its current inputs, with no memory of past inputs.
It consists only of combinational logic gates, without any feedback paths.
It possesses memory elements (like flip-flops) that store the current state, influencing future outputs.
It is primarily used for high-speed arithmetic computations.
It can only exist as a software construct and not as a hardware implementation.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the fundamental operational principle of a Vector Processor in computer architecture?
options: It executes different instructions on different data streams concurrently.
It executes a single instruction on an entire array of data elements (vectors) simultaneously or in a pipelined fashion.
It relies heavily on out-of-order execution and speculative branching to maximize instruction-level parallelism.
It exclusively uses a complex instruction set with variable-length instructions.
It has multiple, independent ALUs operating on scalar operands.
